action.reasoning: Observation: The best official mIoU is 0.5132 (iteration 16, multi-scale resize TTA). Iteration 17's sliding-window approach dropped to 0.4858, confirming that resize-based multi-scale TTA is superior for this model. We have ~70 minutes and 13 iterations left. Analysis: The most reliable way to improve is to optimize the inference pipeline: (1) batch images by similar resized dimensions at each scale to dramatically improve GPU utilization and cut wall-clock time, (2) add an extra high-res scale (2.25) while keeping the full set, and (3) raise max_size to 4000 so high scales preserve more detail on large images. Batching should make 8 scales (16 passes) faster than the unbatched 7 scales (14 passes) from iteration 16. Decision: Rewrite predict.py with a batched multi-scale resize TTA pipeline using 4-GPU spawn. Do NOT touch model.py or train.py to preserve checkpoint compatibility. Run a quick syntax/model-load check, then launch full inference, and submit eval immediately.
action.command: python -c "from model import SegmentationModel; import torch; m=SegmentationModel(num_classes=150); print('Model OK, params', sum(p.numel() for p in m.parameters()))" && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output predictions.json
